fills: 4.1.1.4 — custom-vs-pyKT DKT/SAKT overall AUC (baseline provenance / are the custom deep baselines under-powered?)
generator_script: research_validation/scripts/compare_pykt_vs_custom.py (supersedes scripts/probe_pykt_dkt_sakt.py, which had an eval-range confound)
method: All four models scored through the same matched harness: same 10 held-out eval users, same predict-before-observe protocol, same full-history range, train sets disjoint from eval (--exclude-users). Custom checkpoints reproduce the sealed numbers exactly (custom DKT 0.589, custom SAKT 0.573), so this IS the sealed harness — clean apples-to-apples.

cold_start_window_auc.note: On cold-start windows the custom models read HIGHER than reference pyKT (w5 / w10 / w20).

verdict: The custom deep baselines are NOT under-powered. custom DKT ties reference pyKT DKT; custom SAKT exceeds reference pyKT SAKT; on cold-start windows custom > pyKT for both. The library is not a confound. The earlier '+0.08 pyKT' claim (2026-06-16 first pass) was an artifact of three stacked confounds (eval-range mismatch s[:100] vs full-history; train/test leakage of 3 of 10 eval users; train/eval regime mismatch), all since fixed.

honest_ranking_at_this_scale.note: Overall AUC, this data scale (Junyi-graph matched eval), with reference-grade deep models included.
honest_ranking_at_this_scale.ordering: BKT 0.612 ~= HCIE 0.609 > custom DKT 0.589 ~= pyKT DKT 0.584 > custom SAKT 0.573 ~= GKT 0.571 > pyKT SAKT 0.548
honest_ranking_at_this_scale.hcie_value_note: This ranking uses the 3-learner HCIE 0.609 from the full-Junyi pyKT protocol. The matched-eval headline is the lagged-Kalman HCIE 0.6051 (tie-aware; pass1) on a DIFFERENT 10-user matched protocol — not directly comparable to this ranking's BKT 0.612. State both; do not conflate protocols or claim cross-protocol leadership.

framing: AUC competitive not superior. The point of 4.1.1.4 is robustness to the critique 'your deep baselines are weak': custom DKT/SAKT match/exceed reference pyKT, so HCIE tying BKT and leading every deep model (custom AND reference) survives. Deep KT clusters near chance at pure cold-start because it cannot learn from ~20 cold-start users — a data-scarcity finding, NOT an implementation artifact. ADC-first stands: HCIE's contribution is interpretability + the ADC instrument + leading the KT family at cold-start, not predictive SOTA over data-rich deep KT.
reference_baselines_wired_status: Reference pyKT DKT/SAKT now wired as dkt_pykt / sakt_pykt baselines so an end-to-end re-run can include reference-grade deep models. Stated as available-but-not-re-run-here per the no-new-run discipline.
